seo.title: Sample Inspection Reports MA | Ken Arnold Home Inspection
seo.description: See what a Ken Arnold inspection report looks like. Sample single-family and condo reports — photo-documented, organized by priority, with digital delivery.

sections.heading: Sample Inspection Reports
sections.subheading: See the Deliverable Before You Book
sections.intro: Two redacted sample reports — single-family and condo — showing exactly what a Ken Arnold inspection report looks like: the structure, the level of photo detail, how findings are prioritized, and the practical recommendations that accompany each item.

sections.heading: Two Report Formats
sections.intro: Reports are redacted — addresses, owner names, and identifying photos removed. Findings are real.

sections.heading: How to Interpret

paragraphs.html: A first-time buyer's natural reaction to a home inspection report is sometimes alarm — there's a lot of detail, and the photographs make every imperfection look consequential. A few things to keep in mind:

checklist.text: Findings are graded by priority — safety items (missing GFCI, knob-and-tube wiring, asbestos disclosure) at the top, cosmetic items at the bottom.
Every property has findings. A 60-page report on a well-maintained home is a thorough report — the page count reflects inspection depth, not property condition.
"Further evaluation recommended" is a signal to bring in a specialist (electrician, structural engineer, HVAC contractor) — not a defect by itself.
After closing, the report becomes a maintenance map — boiler age, roof replacement timeline, shut-off locations. Keep it.

sections.footnote: Questions about a specific finding or the inspection process? See the full FAQ or call Ken directly at (413) 522-8004.

sections.heading: Ready to Schedule
